data.title: Elong Power receives Nasdaq notification of non-compliance
data.description:
• Elong Power Holding Limited (NASDAQ:ELPW) on Thursday said that it has received a notification from Nasdaq regarding non-compliance with several listing rules.
  • The company’s ordinary shares have traded below the minimum closing bid price of $1.00 per share for 30 consecutive business days, in violation of Nasdaq Listing Rule 5450[a][1].
  • Additionally, the company’s Market Value of Listed Securities (MVLS) has fallen below the required $50 million under Nasdaq Listing Rule 5450[b][2][A], and its Market Value of Publicly Held Shares (MVPHS) is below the minimum $15 million as per Nasdaq Listing Rule 5450[b][2][C].
  • The notification does not affect the company's listing or trading on Nasdaq at this time.
  • Elong Power has until April 1, 2026, to regain compliance with the bid price, MVLS, and MVPHS requirements.
  • The company intends to monitor these metrics and take appropriate actions to meet Nasdaq’s continued listing standards.
  • ELPW -1.00% after hours to $0.4222.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: TaskUs terminates take-private deal with Blackstone
data.description:
TaskUS (NASDAQ:TASK) officially terminated its planned sale to Blackstone (BX) after a shareholder vote failed to gain enough support. TaskUs fell 1.2% in after-hours trading after plunging 12% on Wednesday.

TaskUS entered into a mutual agreement to terminate the merger agreement, according to an 8-K filing  on Thursday.  No termination fee is payable by either party in connection with the termination agreement.

On Wednesday, TaskUs (NASDAQ:TASK) said it expected the deal would be terminated after the shareholder vote failed.  The shareholder vote had been adjourned a few times in order to gain enough shareholder support for a deal. Proxy adviser ISS had said holders should reject the offer and some investors had said they plan to reject the offer at its price of $16.50 a share.

Blackstone (NYSE:BX), TaskUs (NASDAQ:TASK) Chief Executive Officer Bryce Maddock, and President Jaspar Weir agreed in May to take private the digital services provider in an all-cash transaction for $16.50 a share.

data.title: Levi Strauss reports another strong quarter, raises FY25 guidance
data.description:
Levi Strauss (NYSE:LEVI) reported better-than-expected results for the third quarter and raised its outlook for the year despite expectations for the macroeconomic climate to remain “complex” and higher-than-anticipated tariffs on imported merchandise.  

“With strength across channels, segments and categories, we are raising our full-year outlook and are well-positioned for the holiday season,” CEO Michelle Gass said, adding that the consistency of the company’s performance and operational agility “gives me the confidence that we will deliver sustained, profitable growth into 2026 and beyond.”

Led by strength in its direct-to-consumer channel, the 172-year old company realized a 7% increase in sales to $1.54B, beating the consensus estimate of $1.50B. Sales were up across all regions with the Americas showing a 6% gain, Europe up 5%, and sales in Asia climbing 12%, all of which were better than anticipated.

Thanks to higher prices, which helped offset the impact of tariffs, gross margin increased 100 basis points to 61.7% versus 60.7% estimates, while operating margin swelled to 10.8% from 2.3% in the same quarter last year, but less than 11.3% estimates.

Adjusted earnings of $0.34 came in 3 cents above expectations.

For the remainder of the year, Levi’s (NYSE:LEVI) raised its FY25 guidance and now expects to earn a profit between $1.27 to $1.32 per share from prior guidance of $1.25 to $1.30, with the midpoint of the new guidance 2 cents better than expected.

Organic net revenue growth was increased to +6% from +4.5% - +5.5%, previously.

The company also raised its outlook for gross margin expansion to +100 basis points from +80 basis points, while its adjusted EBIT margin remains unchanged at 11.4% to 11.6%.

This outlook assumes no worsening of macroeconomic pressures on the consumer, inflationary pressures, supply chain disruptions, and no retaliatory tariff actions.

data.title: Elastic announces $500M share repurchase program
data.description:
• Elastic (NYSE:ESTC) on Thursday announced a share repurchase program under which the company is authorized to repurchase up to $500 million of the company’s outstanding ordinary shares from time to time.
  • The repurchase program does not have an expiration date.
  • ESTC +10.31% after hours to $89.96.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Nabors Industries announces receipt of funds in repayment of seller note from superior energy
data.description:
• Nabors Industries (NYSE:NBR) announced that it has received prepayment in full of the $250M seller financing note tied to its earlier sale of Quail Tools, LLC, to Superior Energy Services.
  • Superior Energy settled the seller note ahead of maturity, strenthening Nabor's liquidity position.
  • The net proceeds will be directed towards debt reduction, particularly its outstanding notes maturing in 2028, and reflecting the company's commitment to enhance long-term shareholder value.

data.title: Torrent Capital reports preliminary Q3 total comprehensive income of C$4.8 million
data.description:
• Torrent Capital (TSXV:TORR:CA) announced on Thursday its preliminary total comprehensive income for Q3 was approximately C$4.8 million, representing a 208% increase from the comparative prior year quarter. All figures reported are net of taxes and operating expenses.
  • As at September 30, 2025, Torrent had a NAV of C$33.5 million (or C$0.88 per share) compared with C$28.7 million (or C$0.75 per share) at June 30, 2025.
  • The C$0.13 per share increase in NAV represents a 17% gain during the third quarter, outperforming the S&P500 which increased 8%, and trailing the TSX Small Cap Index which rose 20% over the same period.
  • During the third quarter, the price of Solana (SOL) appreciated, contributing to Torrent's outperformance as the largest holding in its portfolio. Since establishing a digital asset portfolio in January 2025, staking Solana has generated a yield of 1,725 SOL tokens, representing approximately C$396,000 in value.
  • During the quarter, Torrent recorded realized gains of approximately C$1.2 million from dispositions of its SOL holdings and an unrealized gain of approximately C$2.2 million. As of September 30, 2025, Torrent maintains long exposure to approximately 30,000 SOL through a combination of staked holdings and option contracts.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: U.S. targets Chinese firms, tankers in latest round of Iran oil sanctions
data.description:
The United States announced new sanctions Thursday against roughly 100 people, companies and vessels accused of helping facilitate Iran’s oil and petrochemical trade, including Chinese refineries and shipping operators, Reuters reported.

Among those designated was Shandong Jincheng Petrochemical Group, an independent “teapot” refinery in Shandong province that U.S. officials say has imported millions of barrels of Iranian crude since 2023. The Treasury Department also blacklisted the Rizhao Shihua Crude Oil Terminal at Lanshan Port, which it said received shipments from more than a dozen tankers tied to Iran’s shadow fleet (including vessels such as Kongm, Big Mag and Voy) that move sanctioned oil in violation of restrictions.

Washington argues that revenue from these networks bankrolls Tehran’s nuclear and missile programs and supports allied militant groups across the Middle East. Iran insists its nuclear activities are civilian in nature.

The action marked the fourth round of U.S. sanctions aimed at Chinese buyers of Iranian crude, highlighting a broader push by the Trump administration to choke off Iran’s energy exports. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the measures are designed to “degrade Iran’s cash flow by dismantling critical parts of its export system.”

The crackdown came as Israel and Hamas reached a ceasefire and hostage agreement, the most promising effort yet to end the conflict in Gaza, which has expanded into a regional confrontation involving Iran, Lebanon and Yemen.

President Donald Trump, speaking after a White House cabinet meeting, noted that Iran signaled support for the truce. He added that while the U.S. would like to see Iran focus on rebuilding, “they cannot be allowed to obtain a nuclear weapon.” Trump also said he plans to travel to the Middle East soon.

Separately, the State Department sanctioned Jiangyin Foreversun Chemical Logistics, the first China-based terminal targeted for receiving Iranian petrochemical cargoes.

Neither the Chinese embassy in Washington nor Iran’s U.N. mission in New York responded immediately to requests for comment, Reuters reported.

data.title: Amcor appoints Stephen Scherger as CFO
data.description:
• Amcor (NYSE:AMCR) (ASX:AMC) has appointed Stephen R. Scherger as Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer, effective November 10, 2025.
  • Scherger succeeds Michael Casamento, who is leaving the company to return to Australia after a decade as CFO.
  • Casamento will remain in an advisory role until June 30, 2026.
  • Scherger brings over 30 years of experience, most recently serving as CFO at Graphic Packaging, where he played a key role in the company’s growth and integration of large acquisitions.
  • Amcor also reaffirmed its fiscal 2026 outlook, expecting adjusted EPS growth of 12-17% and free cash flow of $1.8-1.9 billion.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Codelco reports lowest copper output in over 20 years after fatal mine accident
data.description:
Codelco’s copper production fell to its weakest monthly level in more than two decades after a deadly mine collapse deepened the Chilean state miner’s long-running struggles to reverse declining output, Bloomberg News reported Thursday.

The company produced 93,400 metric tons in August, down 25% from a year earlier and the lowest figure since Chile’s copper commission Cochilco began tracking data in 2003.

The sharp drop followed a July 31 accident at the El Teniente mine that killed six workers, injured nine others, and forced a shutdown lasting more than a week. Although some operations have since resumed in unaffected areas, the incident (the country’s most serious mining disaster in 30 years) already led Codelco to cut its annual production guidance, further challenging its position as the world’s largest copper producer.

The disruption adds to tightening global supply concerns just as demand for copper climbs, driven by the clean energy transition and the rapid buildout of data centers requiring vast amounts of electrical wiring.

Other major Chilean mines posted mixed results in August. BHP’s (BHP) Escondida, the world’s biggest copper operation, produced 105,100 tons, slightly below July’s output but roughly in line with the same month last year. Collahuasi, jointly owned by Anglo American (OTCQX:AAUKF) (OTCQX:NGLOY) and Glencore (OTCPK:GLCNF) (OTCPK:GLNCY), recorded a modest month-over-month increase, though volumes remain significantly lower than last year due to lower ore grades.

data.title: Stephen Scherger resigns as Graphic Packaging's CFO
data.description:
• Graphic Packaging (NYSE:GPK) said on Thursday that Stephen Scherger has resigned as executive vice president and chief financial officer, effective as of November 7
  • Charles Lischer will take over as senior vice president and interim CFO following Scherger's departure.
  • Lischer has served as the firm's senior vice president and chief accounting officer since November 2019. Prior to joining the company, Lischer served as the senior vice president, finance and chief accounting officer for Teradata, and prior to that in various finance and accounting positions at The Coca-Cola Company and its affiliated companies.
  • GPK -1.99% to $17.77 after market.
